findings.description: Back-import from sibling behavioral module: marketmind.pipeline.layer1_narrative (line 10). PICA §3.1 Rule 3 prohibits imports from sibling behavioral modules. Import from config.settings (different layer, line 11) is compliant.
findings.remediation: ACCEPTED AS TEMPORARY — this is a compatibility adapter by design. Per docstring: 'New callers should use layer1_narrative directly.'
findings.acceptance_rationale: Compatibility adapter wrapping layer1_narrative for the older interactive API.

findings.description: InteractiveState and ToolState dataclasses (lines 14-29) are exported but not used within this module. They are defined for external consumers but represent dead weight in this file.
findings.remediation: Consider moving ToolState and InteractiveState to session_context.py or a shared types module if they are consumed elsewhere.

summary: One import boundary violation — back-import from layer1_narrative (sibling behavioral module), accepted as intentional for compatibility adapter role. Data flow is well-defined: config → analyze_layer1 → (Layer1Result, bool, extras). Single entry point (run_l1_interactive) with clear inputs (MarketMindConfig, mock flag, verbose flag, shadow_count) and output (tuple[Layer1Result, bool, dict]). No control flow hazards — straight-line code with exception handling.
